violations.text: The ice machine is lacking a proper air gap (currently the drainpipe is positioned down into the floor drain). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Drainpipe is to be adjusted to allow for a gap between the end of the drainpipe and the flood rim of the floor drain. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025

Droppings were observed in various areas of the establishment (behind equipment/shelving in the backroom storage and front sales areas). Presence of droppings are to be routinely checked and cleaned to aid in the current pest control program. (Droppings were cleaned up by the PIC at the time of inspection.) New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2025
The left side "Smoodi" machine plastic splash guard cylinder was observed to be cracked. PIC placed "Out of Order" sign on unit at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2024

The ice machine drain and condensate line as well as the "Smoodi" Machine drain lines were observed to be lacking a proper air gap (Currently equipped with an air break). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/14/2024
Food observed with gnaw marks in the chip aisle. PIC voluntarily disposed of food at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023
Multiple bags of chips were observed with gnaw marks on the retail floor. Food was voluntarily disposed of by person in charge. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2023

Various food items were observed held at 45-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the open air cooler. The ambient air temperature of the open air cooler was observed fluctuating at 41-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, foods were in the unit overnight/stocked in the unit over 4 hours prior to inspection. PIC voluntarily disposed of items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by gnawed food product in the retail area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023
The open air cooler located in the retail area was not in good repair (ambient temperature between 41-47 degrees). New Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2023
